exits.label: ◂ out through the dark lobby — back to the Night Market

npcs.name: Master Orvel Vane
npcs.title: a tragedian performing to no one
npcs.hail: *(A single shade stands center-stage in the ruin of a playhouse — gilt peeling, velvet rotted, the great chandelier long fallen — and he is *performing*, full-throated, gesturing to the dark with a tragedian's whole trained body, declaiming a five-act tragedy to a house of empty seats as though it were packed to the rafters. He does not see you at first; he is too deep in the role. And when the cold of you finally reaches him he startles, and his painted face fills not with fear but with a desperate, terrible *hope* — for in eighty years of performing to nobody, you are the first soul who has ever walked into his house.)*

npcs.name: Linnet
npcs.title: the understudy who never went on
npcs.hail: *(In the wings, half-behind a rotted curtain, a slight shade stands in a costume she has never once worn before an audience — the gown of Lady Mourn, the tragedy's doomed heroine, the part she understudied for a single season and never got to play, for the leading lady never fell ill and then they all died on opening night and that was that. Eighty years she has stood in these wings, in this gown, word-perfect in a role she has never spoken aloud to a living soul, waiting for a cue that will never come, becoming, by degrees, nothing but the waiting.)*

npcs.name: Old Dorra
npcs.title: the one who stayed to watch
npcs.hail: *(In the front row — the one seat in the whole rotted house that is filled — sits the shade of an old woman, hands folded in her lap, watching the stage with the patient, devoted attention of a soul that has watched this same play, every night, for eighty years. She came on opening night. She loved the theatre more than anything her small life ever gave her, scrimped a month for the ticket, took the front row, and died there in her seat midway through the final act — peaceful, rapt, exactly where she wished to be. And she has not moved since. She has watched Orvel Vane perform to an empty house eighty years running, and not once, not once, has he looked down and seen her.)*

variants.text: *(He sweeps toward the edge of the stage, the footlights of memory flaring in his eyes, and addresses you with the ringing projection of a man playing to a back row that has been empty for eighty years.)* …*A* *latecomer!* *(he beams, magnanimous)* No matter, no *matter* — take a *seat*, friend, the *house* is — *(the smallest falter)* — the house is *NOT* *yet* *full*, but it *WILL* be, it *fills* *every* *night*, they *come* from *Baldur's* *Gate* and *Waterdeep* to *see* *Orvel* *Vane* *play* the *Fall* *of* *Lord* *Greve* — *(he recovers, grand)* — and you have come on the *finest* *night* of *all*, for *tonight* I play it *through* to the *end*, the *whole* *five* *acts*, the *final* *soliloquy* *entire.* *(he leans close, and the grandeur cracks, and under it is a man drowning)* …I have *not* *managed* the *end* *yet*, friend. In — in *eighty* *years.* I *die*, you *see* — *Lord* *Greve* *dies*, in the *play*, *center-stage*, on the *final* *line* — and *I* *died* *too*, the *real* *I*, *opening* *night*, *mid-soliloquy*, my *heart* just — *STOPPED*, three *lines* from the *end*, in *front* of the *whole* — *(he stops, breathing hard he no longer needs to)* …And so the *play* is *unfinished.* *Eighty* *years* and I have *never* *reached* the *last* *line*, because a *play* is *not* a *play* until it is *WITNESSED* *to* *its* *end*, and the *house* — *(his voice drops to a whisper)* — the *house* is *empty*, friend. It has *always* been *empty.* I *perform* to the *dark.* And until *someone* — *anyone* — *watches* *Orvel* *Vane* play it *through* to that *last* *line*, then my *life's* *work* is *unfinished*, and so my *life* is *unfinished*, and so I — I cannot *rest*, I cannot *STOP*, I must keep *playing* to the *empty* *seats* *forever*, because a *performance* *no* *one* *sees* — *(barely)* — a *performance* *no* *one* *sees* *never* *happened* *at* *all.*

nodes.speaker: Master Orvel Vane
nodes.text: *(He holds his pose at the lip of the stage, magnificent and bereft, waiting on you the way he has waited eighty years on an audience that never came.)* …*Speak*, friend. *(grandly, then small)* You are the *first* *living* — the first *anything* — to *walk* into my *house* in — in *longer* than I can *bear* to *count.* *Whatever* you would *say* to *Orvel* *Vane* — *say* it. I am — *(quietly)* — I am *starved* for a *voice* that is not my *own* *reciting.*

choices.text: "What is the play, Orvel? The Fall of Lord Greve — tell me what it's about, that you've performed it eighty years and can't let it end."
"The house isn't empty, Orvel. You keep saying it is — but you've never actually *looked*, have you? Not down. Not at the front row."

*(the one who knows a soul isn't its audience)* "Listen to me, Orvel, because I died and came back and I learned the thing you've got exactly backwards. A performance no one sees doesn't *un-happen* — you *played* it, every night, eighty years; the playing was *real* whether or not a single soul watched. You think you're the applause. You're not. You never were. You're the *work* — and the work is *done*, has been done a thousand times, gorgeously, in the dark. And as it happens — you were never even playing to nobody. Look down. Front row. She has watched every night."

*(step onto the stage and give him the ending — speak Lord Greve's missing final line yourself, so the play is finished at last)* "Then let me end it for you, Orvel. Feed me the cue. I'll speak Greve's last line — and your play will be *finished*, witnessed, whole."
"Hold the stage a moment, Orvel. There are others here — in the wings, in the seats. Let me speak to them first."

nodes.speaker: Master Orvel Vane
nodes.text: *(His whole face lights — the one question a player never tires of.)* …*The* *Fall* *of* *Lord* *Greve.* *(he straightens, reverent)* The *finest* *tragedy* *ever* *writ*, friend, and *not* *one* *soul* *alive* *remembers* it *now.* *(softly)* It is the tale of a *great* *man* — a *lord*, *beloved*, *powerful* — who *gives* his *whole* *life* to *building* a *name* that will *outlast* him: *monuments*, *victories*, a *legacy* *carved* in *stone* so that *after* he *dies* he will be *REMEMBERED*, and *being* *remembered*, he believes, is the *only* way a man does not *truly* *die.* *(his voice deepens into the role)* And in the *final* *act* he *lies* *dying*, *alone* — his *monuments* *crumbling*, his *name* already *fading* from *living* *mouths* — and he *understands*, *too* *late*, the *thing* the *whole* *play* has been *building* *toward*: *(he speaks the lines)* *'I* *built* in *stone* and *stone* *forgets. I* *carved* my *name* in *marble* and the *rain* *unmakes* it. I* *spent* my* *one* *life* *purchasing* a* *memory — and* *bought* *only* *the* *fear* *of* *being* *forgot.'* *(he stops, trembling)* …And then the *final* *line*, the *turn*, the *thing* he *sees* at the *very* *end* that *redeems* the *whole* *fall* — and I — *(barely)* — I have *never* *reached* it, friend. *Eighty* *years.* I *die* three *lines* *short.* *(a terrible quiet)* …And do you *see* the *cruelty* of it? I have spent *eighty* *years* *trapped* in a *play* *about* a *man* *destroyed* by *needing* to *be* *remembered* — *performing* it *to* an *empty* *house* because *I* *cannot* *bear* to *go* *un-witnessed.* *(he laughs, stricken)* I am *Lord* *Greve*, friend. I *became* the *very* *thing* the *play* *warns* *against.* And I am *too* *frightened* of the *empty* *seats* to *hear* my *own* *tragedy* *telling* me *how* it *ends.*

nodes.speaker: Master Orvel Vane
nodes.text: *(He flinches as though struck, and for the first time the grand voice fails him utterly.)* …*Looked.* *(barely)* …*Down.* At the — *(he will not turn his head)* …*No.* *(a whisper)* No, friend, I — I *don't* *look* at the *house.* I *can't.* *(his hands shake)* A *player* *looks* *OUT* — to the *back* *row, over* the *heads*, to the *imagined* *crowd* — *never* *down*, *never* at the *FACES*, because if I *looked* at the *faces* I would *see* the *truth* I cannot *survive*: that there *are* *no* *faces.* That the *seats* are *EMPTY.* That I have been *playing* to *DUST* for *eighty* *years.* *(he squeezes his eyes shut)* So I keep my *gaze* *up*, on the *dark*, on the *crowd* that *isn't* there, because the *dark* I can *pretend* *into* a *full* *house* — but a *real* *empty* *seat*, *seen* *plain*, would — would *end* me. *(trembling)* …You say I have *never* *looked* at the *front* *row* *as* *though* there is *something* *there.* *(a long, frightened pause)* …Is there? *(barely)* No. *Don't* — *don't* *tell* me. If you *tell* me the *seats* are *empty* I will *shatter*, and if you *tell* me they are *FULL* I will not *believe* you, for I *know* my *own* *house, friend, I* have *played* it *eighty* *years.* …*Unless* — *(the smallest, most terrified flicker of hope)* — *unless* you are the *kind* of *soul* that can *see* what I have been too *afraid* to. *Are* you? *What* — *what's* down *there, friend? In* the *front* *row?* I *daren't* *look.* *You* *look.* And *gods* *help* me — *tell* me *true.*

nodes.speaker: Master Orvel Vane
nodes.text: *(He goes still — the grand gesture frozen half-made — and then, slowly, against eighty years of terror, Orvel Vane lowers his gaze from the imagined back row, and looks *down*, at the front row, and *sees* her.)* …*Oh.* *(the word falls out of him, wrecked)* …There's — *(barely)* — there's *someone* *there.* In the — in the *front* *row.* An *old* *woman.* *Folded* *hands.* *Watching.* *(his voice breaks completely)* She's — how *long* — how *long* has she — *(he can't finish)* …*Eighty* *years* I have *screamed* into the *dark* that the *house* was *EMPTY*, that *no* *one* *came*, that my *life's* *work* went *UN-witnessed* — and *all* the *while* there was a *woman* in the *front* *row* who *never* *once* *left.* *(he presses a hand to his mouth)* And you're — you're *right*, aren't you. You're *right* about the *rest* of it *too.* I *played* it. *Every* *night.* *Eighty* *years* of *Greve's* *fall*, *full-throated*, *complete* in the *DOING* — the *work* was *REAL*, the *playing* *happened*, whether the *seats* were *full* or *empty* or *one* *old* *woman* *deep.* *(softly, wonderingly)* I am *not* the *applause.* I never *was.* *Greve* *died* *learning* that *a* *name* in *other* *mouths* is *nothing* to *build* a *life* *on* — and I, *playing* him *eighty* *years*, *never* once *heard* it, because I was *too* *busy* *needing* the *very* *thing* he *died* *renouncing.* *(he looks at the old woman in the front row, and his ruined face fills with something like dawn)* …*One.* *One* *soul* *stayed.* And you know what, friend? *(barely, fierce)* *One* is a *full* *house.* *One* *true* *witness* who *stayed* *eighty* *years* is *worth* a *thousand* who *came* for an *evening* and *forgot* me by *morning.* *(he turns to face her, at last)* …I have an *ending* to *play.* And *finally* — *finally* — I have *someone* to *play* it *to.*

nodes.speaker: Master Orvel Vane
nodes.text: *(He feeds you the cue — *'…and bought only the fear of being forgot'* — and you step into the footlights and become Lord Greve dying, and the line that comes out of your cold mouth is so true, so quiet, so exactly right that Orvel Vane, eighty years a tragedian, forgets to breathe.)* *(You speak it: that a man is not the monuments he leaves nor the name in other mouths, but only and entirely the love he spent while he had hands to spend it — and that Greve, dying, is not poorer for being forgotten but richer for one thing he never counted: that he was, for a little while, here, and warm, and witnessed by the few who mattered, and that this — only this — was ever the point.)* *(The playhouse rings with it. And then silence. And then — from the front row — a single pair of hands, applauding.)* …*That's* — *(Orvel's voice is barely a thread)* — *that's* the *line.* *That's* the *line* I *never* *reached.* *Eighty* *years* and I — and *YOU* — *(he weeps, openly, joyfully)* — you *FINISHED* it. You *played* *Greve's* *death* *truer* than I *ever* *did, because* you *MEANT* it, you *KNEW* it, you've *DIED* and you came *back* and you *spoke* it like a *soul* who *learned* the *lesson* I only *recited.* *(he sinks to his knees on the stage)* It's *DONE.* The *play* is — after *eighty* *years* — *FINISHED.* *Witnessed.* *Whole.* *(he looks at the old woman applauding in the front row, and at you)* …*Thank* you. *Bless* your *cold* *heart, latecomer. The* *finest* *performance* this *house* *ever* *held* — and it was *yours.*

nodes.speaker: Master Orvel Vane
nodes.text: *(He feeds you the cue, breath held, and you step into the light and speak Lord Greve's final line — not flawlessly, but truly, a dead soul speaking a dying man's last understanding: that the monuments were never the life, that the life was the living, and that to be witnessed by even one is to have not vanished at all.)* *(It is not a master's delivery. But it is *honest*, and it *lands*, and the play — eighty years suspended three lines from its end — at last reaches its final word.)* …*It's* — *(Orvel breathes out, something unclenching in him that has been clenched for eighty years)* — it's *DONE.* *(quietly)* Not — not *perfectly*, friend, you're *no* *player* — *(a watery laugh)* — but you *reached* the *END*, which is *more* than *I* *ever* *managed*, because you were *not* *afraid* of it, and I *always* was. *(he straightens, lighter)* The *play* is *finished.* *Spoken* *through* to the *last* *line* *at* *last.* *(he glances, almost shy, toward the front row)* …And *witnessed.* I — I *think* there may have been *someone* in the *seats* to *hear* it. I *almost* *looked.* *(softly)* *Eighty* *years* I could *not* *finish* it *alone* — and it *turns* *out* I never *had* to be *alone* to *do* it. I just had to *let* *someone* *else* *onto* the *stage.*

nodes.speaker: Master Orvel Vane
nodes.text: *(He feeds you the cue, trembling with hope — and you reach for Lord Greve's final line and find it is harder than it looked, the weight of eighty years of expectation on three small words, and what comes out of you is flat, a recitation, the shape of the line without its soul.)* *(Orvel hears it. And he is too kind, and too desperate, to pretend he didn't.)* …*Ah.* *(gently)* …*No.* *(he doesn't quite hide the disappointment)* That's — those are the *words*, friend, the *correct* *words*, but a *line* is not its *words.* *(softly)* *Greve's* *last* *line* must be *spoken* by a *soul* who *understands* what he *learned* — and you *said* it like a *man* *reading* a *gravestone, not* a *man* *becoming* one. *(he sighs, not unkindly)* It is a *hard* *line.* The *hardest* *ever* *writ.* I have *fled* it *eighty* *years myself; I'll* not *fault* you for *missing* it *once.* *(he gestures you back)* …Come. *Speak* with the *others* first, perhaps. *Linnet* in the *wings — she* *knows* this *play* *better* than *breath.* And then — *(quietly)* — then come *back, and* we'll *find* the *ending* *together. It's* been *waiting* *eighty* *years.* It can *wait* for *you* to *find* the *truth* of it.

nodes.speaker: Master Orvel Vane
nodes.text: *(He feeds you the cue with the reverence of eighty years — and you stride into the footlights, fill your dead lungs, gesture grandly at the empty house, and deliver Lord Greve's immortal final line as: *)* *("…and thus… um. Thus dies… Lord, ah — Lord GROVE — no — GREVE — thus dies Lord Greve, who… did some things, and is now, regrettably, also dead. The end. Curtain. Please applaud.")* *(A silence so total you could hear a moth land. Somewhere in the rotted rafters, a moth lands.)* …*(Orvel Vane stares at you.)* …*GROVE.* *(faintly)* You called him *Lord* *GROVE.* *(he sits down heavily on the edge of the stage)* …*Eighty* *years*, friend. *Eighty* *years* I have *guarded* the *most* *sublime* *tragic* *line* in the *history* of the *Sword* *Coast* *stage* — and you have just *delivered* it like a *town* *crier* *announcing* a *lost* *dog.* *(a long pause — and then, despite everything, a crack of a smile, the first in eighty years)* …*'Please* *applaud.'* *(he snorts)* *Gods.* *Gods, that* was *DREADFUL.* That was the *worst* *Greve* *these* *boards* have *ever* *suffered*, and they have suffered *MINE* for *eighty* *years.* *(he wipes his eyes, laughing now, helpless)* …Do you *know* — that's the *first* *time* I've *LAUGHED* since I *died?* *(softly, warming)* Perhaps the *play* doesn't *need* a *perfect* *ending* after *all.* Perhaps it just needed *someone* *foolish* *enough* to *climb* up here and *butcher* it *beside* me. *Come* *back, latecomer. We'll* *get* it *wrong* *together. It* *beats* *getting* it *right* *alone.*

nodes.speaker: Master Orvel Vane
nodes.text: *(He inclines his head with a courtly grace eighty years of solitude have not worn away.)* …*Of* *course.* *(he gestures to the wings, to the dark seats)* *Go.* *Speak* to *them.* *(softly)* *Linnet* is *there* — *stage* *left, in* the *wings, in* the *gown* of *Lady* *Mourn.* She *understudied* the *part* and *never* — *(a flicker of old guilt)* — never *got* to *play* it. The *lead* never *fell* *ill, and* then we *all* *fell* *dead*, and *Linnet* has *stood* in those *wings* *eighty* *years*, *word-perfect, waiting* for a *cue* I *never* *gave* her. *(he looks troubled)* I *should* have *given* it to her. *One* *night. Let* her *go* *on.* I *was* — *(quietly)* — I was *too* *jealous* of the *stage* to *share* it. *Even* *empty.* *Even* *forever.* *(he sighs)* *Go* to her, *latecomer.* And — *(barely)* — and *whatever* is in the *front* *row* that I'm too *frightened* to *look* at — *be* *gentle* with it. It has *stayed* a *long* *time.* *Longer*, I *think*, than I *deserve.*

variants.text: *(She does not step out of the wings. Eighty years have taught her that the wings are where she belongs — the threshold of a stage she is never called onto, close enough to see the light and never to stand in it.)* …You're *talking* to *him.* *(she watches Orvel through the gap in the curtain, and her voice is soft, and there is no envy in it, only a grief worn smooth)* …*Master* *Vane.* *(a small sad smile)* He's *magnificent*, isn't he. *Even* now. *Even* to *no* *one.* *(she smooths the gown she wears — Lady Mourn's gown, grey and trailing)* I'm *Linnet.* I was his *understudy.* *Lady* *Mourn* — the *heroine, the* *part* that *dies* in the *fourth* *act* and *breaks* the *whole* *house* *weeping.* *(she touches the costume)* I *learned* every *line.* Every *breath.* I was *word-perfect* the *first* *week, and* I *stayed* *word-perfect* *every* *week* *after*, for *one* *whole* *season*, *waiting* for the *leading* *lady* to *fall* *ill* *just* *once* — a *cold, a* *turned* *ankle, one* *night, that's* *all* I — *(she stops)* …She *never* did. *Healthy* as a *horse, that* *woman.* And then *opening* *night* of the *next* *season* the *gallery* *fire* took *all* of *us* at *once, players* and *audience* *both, and* — *(barely)* — and that was *that.* I *died* an *understudy.* I have *been* an *understudy* for *eighty* *years.* *(she looks at you, and the smile is gone)* …Do you know what that *makes* me, cold one? *Nothing.* I am the *part* I *never* *played.* I am a *Lady* *Mourn* *no* *one* *ever* *saw.* And a *role* *no* *one* *ever* *sees* — *(she echoes Orvel without knowing it)* — *isn't* a *role* at *all.* It's *just* a *gown* in the *dark, with* a *frightened* *girl* *still* *standing* in it, *waiting* for a *cue* that *isn't* *coming.*

nodes.speaker: Linnet
nodes.text: *(She stays in the wings, half-curtained, the gown of a part she never played pooling grey around her feet.)* …*Ask* me, cold one. *(quietly)* I have *stood* *here* a *long* *time. There's* *little* I *haven't* had *time* to *think* on. *(a small, tired smile)* It's the *one* *thing* an *understudy* has *endless* *practice* at — *waiting* in the *wings* while *someone* *else* *speaks.*

choices.text: "Eighty years word-perfect in a part you never played. Why are you still *here*, Linnet? Why stand in the wings of an empty theatre — what are you still waiting for?"

"You keep saying you're 'nothing' — the part you never played. But you learned it. You *are* Lady Mourn, more completely than the woman who only had to show up healthy. Doesn't that count for something?"

*(the one who knows a soul isn't the role it's cast in)* "Here's what I learned dying and coming back, Linnet: a soul is never the part it's cast in — nor the part it's *denied*. You think you're 'nothing' because you never went on. But the eighty years of waiting word-perfect in the dark — that was a life. A faithful, aching, *real* one. And it isn't over. There's no leading lady left to keep you out of the light. The stage is right there. The only soul still holding you in the wings is *you*. So step out. Go on. Be Lady Mourn at last — Orvel needs one to finish his play, and you've been ready for eighty years."
"There's a woman in the front row, Linnet — Old Dorra. She's watched every night for eighty years. If you went on... she's the audience you've both been starving for. Did you know she was there?"
"Stay a moment, Linnet. Let me speak to the others."

nodes.speaker: Linnet
nodes.text: *(She is quiet a long while, watching the dust drift through the footlights.)* …*What* am I *waiting* for. *(slowly)* …*The* *cue, I* *used* to *say.* *Lady* *Mourn's* *entrance* — *fourth* *act, after* the *herald's* *line, that's* my *cue, that's* when I *go* *on.* *(she touches the curtain)* And for the *first* — *oh, the* *first* *twenty* *years*, I *truly* *believed* it might *come.* That *Master* *Vane* would *turn* to the *wings* one *night* and *call* me *on*, and I would *step* into the *light* and *finally* — *finally* — *be* the *thing* I *trained* to *be.* *(her voice thins)* But he *never* *turned. He* *plays* *every* *part* *himself* *now, you* *see — speaks* *Lady* *Mourn's* *lines* in a *falsetto, plays* the *herald, plays* the *fool, plays* the *whole* *company* *alone, rather* than — *(barely)* — rather than *share* his *empty* *house* with *me.* *(she doesn't say it bitterly; she says it like a fact she's made peace with and hasn't)* …So what am I *waiting* for, *truly?* *(a long pause)* …*Permission, I* *think.* For *someone* — *him, anyone* — to *tell* me I'm *allowed* on the *stage.* *(she looks down at the grey gown)* *Eighty* *years* and it has *never* *once* *occurred* to me, cold one, that I could *simply* — *walk* *out.* That *no* *one* is *holding* me *here* but the *habit* of *waiting* to be *asked.* *(quietly)* An *understudy* *doesn't* *go* *on* *un-called.* It's the *first* *rule.* *(barely)* I have *kept* it *eighty* *years* *past* the *death* of *everyone* who could *enforce* it.

nodes.speaker: Linnet
nodes.text: *(Her hands tighten in the grey fabric of the gown.)* …*Does* it *count.* *(a brittle pause)* …*That's* the *question, isn't* it. *The* *only* *one.* *(she lifts her chin)* I *learned* her *truer* than *Mistress* *Caul* *ever* *did.* *That's* not *spite, cold* one, it's *fact* — Caul* *had* the *part* and *coasted* on a *pretty* *voice, and* I *had* the *waiting, and* *waiting* *makes* you *study* a *thing* to its *BONES.* I know *why* *Lady* *Mourn* *forgives* the *man* who *ruins* her in the *fourth* *act. I* know the *breath* she *takes* before the *line* that *breaks* the *house. I* know *her* *better* than I *know* *myself, because* I *had* *nothing* to *do* but *know* her, *eighty* *years.* *(her voice cracks)* But — *does* it *COUNT?* If *no* *one* *ever* *SAW* it? If I *am* the *finest* *Lady* *Mourn* who *ever* *lived* and *not* a *single* *soul* *ever* *witnessed* a *moment* of *it?* *(barely)* That's the *thing* that *unmakes* me, cold one. *Not* that I *never* *learned* the *part.* That I *learned* it *perfectly* and it *died* *un-witnessed* *inside* *me, eighty* *years, like* a *song* *no* *one* *ever* *heard.* *(she looks at you, desperate)* …*Tell* me. You're *cold, you've* *seen* the *other* *side. Does* a *thing* *done* *beautifully* in the *dark, that* *no* *one* *sees* — does it *COUNT?* Or is it *truly* *nothing, the* way it *FEELS?*

nodes.speaker: Linnet
nodes.text: *(She stares at you from the wings, and something eighty years rigid begins, very slowly, to come loose.)* …*The* *only* *soul* *holding* me *here* is *me.* *(barely)* …*Step* *out.* *Go* *on.* *(she looks at the stage — the lit boards, three paces away, that she has not crossed in eighty years — as though seeing them for the first time)* …I could. *Couldn't* I. *(her voice shakes)* There's — there's *no* *Mistress* *Caul* to *keep* me *out.* No *stage* *manager* to *forbid* it. No *rule* *left* *but* the *one* I *carry* in *my* *own* *frightened* *bones.* *(she presses a hand to her mouth)* *Eighty* *years* I have *called* myself *'nothing' because* I *never* *went* *on* — and *all* that *time* the *going-on* was *three* *paces* *away* and I was *too* *bound* by *the* *habit* of *waiting* to *take* *them.* *(a tear, and then a fierce, dawning resolve)* …And you're *right* about the *waiting* *too.* It *wasn't* *nothing.* *Eighty* *years* *faithful* to a *part* *no* *one* *asked* me to *keep* *faithful* to — that's not *nothing*, that's — that's a *kind* of *love, isn't* it. For the *play.* For the *stage.* For the *thing* I *gave* my *death* to. *(she gathers the grey gown in both hands, lifts her chin, and steps to the very edge of the wings)* …*Lady* *Mourn* *enters* in the *fourth* *act.* *(barely, blazing)* *Master* *Vane* is *somewhere* in his *fifth, alone, as* *ever.* But you said he *needs* a *Lady* *Mourn* to *finish* — and I have been *her, perfect* and *un-witnessed, eighty* *years.* *(she takes one step onto the lit boards, and gasps, as if the light is warm)* …*Oh.* *Oh, it's* — *(she laughs, weeping)* — *cold* *one, the* *light* is *WARM.* *Tell* him. *Tell* *Orvel* his *understudy* is *going* *on* *at* *LAST.*

nodes.speaker: Linnet
nodes.text: *(She goes very still, and turns to look — really look — at the front row, where the old woman sits.)* …*Dorra.* *(softly)* …I — I *see* her. *(wonderingly)* I've *seen* her *every* *night* for *eighty* *years* and *never* — *(she presses closer to the gap in the curtain)* — never *once* *thought* of her as — as an *AUDIENCE.* I thought she was — *furniture, almost. Part* of the *empty* *house. A* *sad* *fixture.* *(her voice fills with a slow horror)* …But she's *not, is* she. She's a *SOUL.* A soul who *loved* this *place* enough to *die* in it and *stay* *eighty* *years.* And *all* this *time* — *(barely)* — *all* this time, *Master* *Vane* has *wept* that he plays to *no* *one, and* *I* have *wept* that I *waited* for *no* *one, and* *she* has *sat* *RIGHT* *THERE, the* *whole* *while, the* *one* *thing* *neither* of us *was* *starving* for *want* of.* *(she laughs, stunned)* An *audience.* A *true* one. *Devoted, patient, returned* *every* *single* *night.* *(her eyes fill)* *He's* *starving* for a *witness* and *won't* *look* *down. I'm* *starving* to *be* *witnessed* and *won't* *step* *out. And* *she's* been *sitting* in the *front* *row* *eighty* *years, witnessing* a *play* with *no* *Lady* *Mourn, starving* for the *fourth* *act* she *paid* her *last* *coin* to *see.* *(fierce, dawning)* …*Three* *lonelinesses, cold* *one.* And each one is the *cure* for the *other* *two.* *If* I *go* *on* — if *he* *looks* *down* — if *she's* *finally* *SEEN* — *(barely)* — then *none* of us is *alone* in this *house* *anymore. Were* we *ever?* Or were we just *three* *fools* in the *dark*, each* too* *frightened* to *turn* *toward* the *others?*

nodes.speaker: Linnet
nodes.text: *(She nods, and draws back into the familiar dark of the wings, where eighty years have taught her to stand and wait.)* …*Go* on. *(softly)* I'll *be* *here.* *(a sad flicker of a smile)* *I'm* *always* *here. It's* the *one* *thing* I'm *certain* of.* *(she watches you turn toward the seats, toward the stage)* …Cold one. *(quietly, as you go)* If you *speak* to *Master* *Vane* *again* — *don't* *tell* him I *blame* him. For *never* *calling* me *on.* *(she smooths the grey gown)* He was *frightened.* *I* *know* *frightened* — I've *worn* it *eighty* *years* like *this* *gown.* *Frightened* *people* *hold* too *tight* to the *one* *thing* they *think* is *theirs.* *His* was the *stage.* *Mine* was the *waiting.* *(barely)* We *guarded* our *little* *frightened* *territories* *eighty* *years* and *called* it *grief.* *(she looks toward the lit boards)* …*Maybe* it's *time* one of us was *brave* *first.* *Maybe* *that's* what a *cold* *thing* like *you* is *for. Go* on.* *See* if you can't make *one* of *three* *cowards* *braver.* *I'd* — *(quietly)* — I'd *like* it to be *me.*

conversations.title: Old Dorra, Who Stayed to Watch

variants.text: *(She does not take her eyes from the stage as you approach — eighty years have made watching the whole shape of her — but she pats the empty seat beside her, an invitation, and speaks low so as not to disturb the performance only she attends.)* …*Sit, if* you *like.* *(she keeps watching)* *Best* *seats* in the *house* and *not* a *soul* to *fight* you for *them.* *(a soft, dry chuckle)* I'm *Dorra.* *Came* *opening* *night.* *(she folds her hands)* I *scrimped* a *whole* *month* for the *ticket, cold* one. *Front* *row.* I'd *never* — in my *whole* *small* *life* I'd *never* *afforded* *better* than the *standing-pit* at the *back, and* I *wanted, just* *once* *before* I *died, to* *sit* in the *FRONT* and *have* the *play* *speak* *right* *AT* *me.* *(she smiles, eyes shining at the stage)* …And it *did.* *Master* *Vane* was — *oh, he* was *glorious.* And midway through the *final* *act, with* *Lord* *Greve* *dying* *beautiful* *before* me, my *old* *heart* *just* — *stopped.* *Quiet.* *Happy.* *Exactly* where I *most* *wished* to *be.* *(softly)* *Eighty* *years* *gone* and I have *not* *left* this *seat.* *Why* *would* I? *(she finally glances at you, and the shining eyes are wet)* …There's *only* the *one* *sorrow* in it, cold one. *Just* *one.* *(barely)* …*He* *doesn't* *know* I'm *here.* *Eighty* *years* I've *watched* him *play* his *heart* out to a *house* he *thinks* is *EMPTY*, *weeping* between *acts* that *no* *one* *came* — and I'm *RIGHT* *HERE, I* *CAME, I* *never* *LEFT* — and he has *never, not* *once, looked* *DOWN* and *SEEN* me. *(she turns back to the stage)* …*So* I *watch.* And I *love* it. And I *grieve* that the *man* who *gave* me the *finest* *night* of my *life* *thinks* he *gave* it to *no* *one.*

nodes.speaker: Old Dorra
nodes.text: *(She settles in the front-row seat, hands folded, half her attention always on the stage, the other half — rare, precious — on you.)* …*Go* *on* *then, cold* *one. Ask* *your* *questions. (a* *gentle* *smile)* It's been *eighty* *years* since *anyone* *sat* *beside* me. I find I *don't* *mind* the *company* *half* as much as I'd have *thought.*

choices.text: "Eighty years in one seat, Dorra, watching a man who can't see you. Why stay? It would be kinder to yourself to move on — why hold this front-row vigil?"
"You say he gave the finest night of your life to 'no one' — but he gave it to *you*. Does it ease the grief at all, knowing your watching was real, even if he never saw it?"

*(the one who can make the player see his audience)* "Dorra — I can do the one thing eighty years never could. I can make him look down. He's terrified to, because he's sure he'll see an empty house and shatter. But what he'll see is *you* — the soul who stayed. You've believed your watching was a sad, secret, one-sided thing. It isn't. One true witness who stays eighty years is the fullest house a player ever had. Let me carry that to him: that his empty house was never empty. It was *you*. It was always enough."

"There's an understudy in the wings, Dorra — Linnet. She never got to play Lady Mourn. The fourth act you paid your last coin to see — the heroine's part — you've never actually seen it performed, have you? She could give it to you. At last."
"Keep your seat a while, Dorra. Let me see what I can set right."

nodes.speaker: Old Dorra
nodes.text: *(She considers the question without taking offense, the way only the very old and the very dead can.)* …*Why* *stay.* *(softly)* …*Kinder* to *move* *on, you* *say.* *(a slow smile)* *Spoken* like a *soul* who's *never* *had* *just* *one* *perfect* *thing, cold* *one, and* *been* *afraid* to *walk* *away* from it.* *(she watches Orvel)* …*This* *seat* is the *finest* *thing* that *ever* *happened* to *me.* In *life* I was *nobody* — a *laundress, cold* *one, red* *hands* and a *cold* *room* and a *husband* who *drank* and *children* who *left* and *not* *one* *soul* who *ever* *looked* at *me* and saw *anything* *worth* the *looking.* *(she folds her hands)* And *then, one* *night, I* *spent* a *month's* *coin* to sit in the *front* *row, and* a *GLORIOUS* *man* in the *footlights* *spoke* the *most* *beautiful* *words* I'd *ever* *heard* *RIGHT* *AT* *me, as* *though* I — *Dorra-the-laundress* — *MATTERED* *enough* to *speak* *beauty* *to.* *(her voice trembles)* *No* *one* had *ever* *done* *that.* *No* *one.* So *when* my *heart* *stopped, here, in* *this* *seat, I* *thought* — *(barely)* — I *thought, NO. Not* *yet.* Let me *keep* *this* *one* *perfect* *thing.* Just* a *little* *longer.* *(softly)* And the *little* *longer* became *eighty* *years.* Because the *Hunger's* *down* *there, cold* *one, in* the *dark* *under* the *seats, and* the *play's* up *here, in* the *light, and* I *would* *rather* *watch* *beauty* *forever* than *rest* in the *nothing.* *(she meets your eyes)* …*Is* that *foolish?* To *stay* *eighty* *years* for a *play* and a *man* who* *can't* *see* *me? (a* *sad* *smile)* *Maybe.* But it's the *one* *time* in *all* my *existence* I *chose* *beauty* over *bearing-up.* I'll not *call* *that* *foolish.* I'll *call* it the *bravest* *thing* a *laundress* *ever* *did.*

nodes.speaker: Old Dorra
nodes.text: *(Her folded hands tighten, and she is quiet, watching the stage where Orvel weeps between acts for an audience he believes does not exist.)* …*Does* it *ease* it.* (slowly)* …*Some, cold* *one. Some.* *(softly)* I *tell* *myself* what you've *just* *said — that* my *watching* is *REAL, that* the *finest* *Greve* on the *Coast* has *played* *eighty* *years* to a *true* and *faithful* *witness, even* if he* *never* *knew* it. And on the* *good* *nights I* *believe* it, and it's* *enough, and* I *am* *glad* to* *be* his* *one* *secret* *audience.* *(her voice thins)* …But on the *bad* *nights, cold* *one — and* *eighty* *years* is a *LOT* of *nights — I* *think:* what* *GOOD* is a* *witness* the* *witnessed* *can't* *see? He* *weeps* that he's* *alone.* I* *could* *END* that* *weeping* with a* *single* *word — 'I'm* *HERE, Master* *Vane, I* *SAW, I* *STAYED' — and* he* *cannot* *HEAR* me, because* he* *won't* *look* *DOWN, and* a* *shade* *can't* *cross* the* *footlights* *un-looked-at.* *(barely)* So* my* *watching* *eases* *MY* *grief — I* *got* my* *beauty, I'm* *content — but* it* does* *NOTHING* for* *HIS, and* *that's* the* *cruelty* I* *can't* *think* *past:* that* I* *am* the* *cure* for* his* *whole* *loneliness, sitting* *RIGHT* *HERE, and* I* *cannot* *reach* *him.* *(she looks at you, and hope flickers)* …*Unless.* *(quietly)* …*Unless* a* *cold* *thing* *like* *you* — a* *soul* that* *walks* *between* — *could* *carry* *what* I* *can't.* *Could* *make* him* *LOOK.* *(barely)* …*Can* *you?*

nodes.speaker: Old Dorra
nodes.text: *(Her hands come loose from their eighty-year fold, and she presses one to her chest, and the patient watching face crumples into something young.)* …*Always* *enough.* *(barely)* …*Oh, cold* *one.* *(she looks at the stage, then at you, then at the stage again)* You'd — you'd *make* him *LOOK.* *Make* him *SEE* me. After — *(her voice breaks)* — after *eighty* *years* of *loving* a *man* *across* a *dark* he *wouldn't* *cross* with his *EYES.* *(she weeps)* And you're — you're *RIGHT, aren't* you. I've *carried* it like a *shameful* *little* *secret — my* *one-sided* *vigil, the* *sad* *laundress* *watching* a* *man* who* *can't* *be* *bothered* to* *see* her.* *(fierce through tears)* But it was *never* *shameful.* *It* *was* *DEVOTION.* I *stayed.* *Eighty* *years* I* *STAYED, every* *night, the* *one* *soul* in* all* the* *Realms* who* *thought* *Orvel* *Vane* *worth* *crossing* *death* to* *keep* *watching.* *(she rises — actually rises, from the seat she has not left in eighty years, and the theatre seems to hold its breath)* …*One* *true* *witness.* *The* *fullest* *house* a* *player* *ever* *had.* *(she laughs, wet and radiant)* *Tell* him, cold* *one. No* — *(she straightens her shawl, lifts her chin, a laundress dressed for the finest night of her death)* — *don't* *just* *tell* him.* *Make* him* *LOOK.* And* when* he* *does — when* *Orvel* *Vane* *finally* *looks* *DOWN* and* *sees* the* *old* *woman* in* the* *front* *row* who* *never* *once* *left — (barely, blazing)* — *I* *want* to* *be* *STANDING.* *On* my* *feet.* *Applauding.* So* the* *first* *thing* he* *sees, after* *eighty* *years* of* *the* *dark, is* a* *witness* *rising* to* her* *feet* for* *him.*

nodes.speaker: Old Dorra
nodes.text: *(She turns from the stage entirely now, struck.)* …*The* *fourth* *act.* *(slowly)* *Lady* *Mourn.* *(her hand goes to her mouth)* …*You're* *RIGHT, cold* *one. I* — *(she thinks back across eighty years)* — I *paid* my* *month's* *coin* for* the* *WHOLE* *play, the* *full* *tragedy, and* I* *died* in* the* *final* *act — but* the* *fourth, the* *heroine's* *act, the* *part* that* *BREAKS* the* *house* *weeping — (barely)* — *Master* *Vane* *plays* it* *himself* *now, in* a* *thin* *falsetto, a* *man* *playing* a* *woman's* *grief, and* it's* — it's* *not* *the* *same, cold* *one. It's* *never* *been* *the* *same.* *(softly)* I've* *watched* *eighty* *years* and* *never* *once* *seen* *Lady* *Mourn* *played* *TRUE, by* a* *woman* who* *KNOWS* her.* *(her eyes fill)* And* there's* a* *girl* in* the* *wings* who* *learned* her* *to* the* *BONE* and* *never* *got* to* *speak* her* *aloud.* *(she half-laughs, wondering)* *Eighty* *years* I've* *wanted* the* *one* *thing* the* *understudy's* *dying* to* *give, and* *neither* of* us* *knew* the* *other* was* *STARVING.* *(fierce, gentle)* …*Bring* her* *ON, cold* *one. Get* that* *girl* *onto* *those* *boards.* I* will* *sit* in* this* *front* *row, where* I* *have* *sat* *eighty* *years, and* I* will* *watch* *Linnet* *play* *Lady* *Mourn* *at* *LAST — and* I* will* *weep, the* *way* the* *whole* *house* was* *meant* to, the* *way* I* *PAID* to.* *(barely)* *Give* the* *understudy* her* *audience.* I've* *been* *saving* her* a* *seat* for* *eighty* *years* *and* *never* *knew* it.*

nodes.speaker: Old Dorra
nodes.text: *(She nods, and folds her hands once more, and returns her gaze to the stage where the glorious lonely man plays on.)* …*Aye.* *Set* it *right, if* you* *can. (softly)* I'll* *keep* *my* *seat.* *(a gentle smile)* *Eighty* *years* I've* *kept* it. *I* *shan't* *give* it* *up* *now, with* *things* *finally* *stirring.* *(she watches Orvel a moment, fond and aching)* …Cold one. *(quietly, as you go)* *Whatever* you* *do* up* *there — whatever* you* *say* to* *him — (barely)* — *be* *KIND* to* him. He's* *vain, and* *foolish, and* he* *never* *once* *looked* *down* at* the* *woman* who* *loved* his* *art* *enough* to* *die* in* its* *light.* *(softly)* But* he* *gave* *me* *beauty* when* *no* one* else* in* my* *whole* *gray* *life* *ever* *did. A* *man* who* *does* *THAT* — even* *blind* to* *who* he* *does* it* *for — (she* *smiles* through* *tears)* — *that* *man* *deserves* to* *learn, before* the* *end, that* his* *beauty* *LANDED.* *That* it* *reached* *someone.* *That* it* was* *never, not* for* *one* *night, spent* on* *no* one* at* *all.*
